Bundle code in scripts/, docs in references/ (REFERENCE.md, FORMS.md), templates/data in assets/; reference files one level deep using relative forward-slash paths; reference files >100 lines start with a table of contents.

Write descriptions in third person with specific trigger keywords; models under-trigger so enumerate when-to-use contexts; keep description on one logical line (wrapping breaks discovery); prefer gerund (processing-pdfs) or noun-phrase names, avoid helper/utils/tools.

Prefer pre-made utility scripts over generated code; handle errors explicitly ('solve, don't punt'); use plan-validate-execute and validator->fix->repeat feedback loops; no time-sensitive info in the body.
Validate with the skills-ref library: `skills-ref validate ./my-skill`; develop eval-first (~3 evals, no-skill baseline) and test across Haiku/Sonnet/Opus.
when_to_use: Whenever authoring, editing, or auditing a SKILL.md on any surface (Claude Code, API, claude.ai, Agent SDK): deciding frontmatter fields, structuring the skill directory, applying progressive disclosure, bundling scripts/resources, or debugging why a skill fails to trigger/validate.

install: Open-source skill in anthropics/skills; copy skills/skill-creator/ into your skills dir. Claude Code: /plugin install skill-creator@claude-plugins-official then /reload-plugins.
quickstart: In Claude Code: 'create a skill that ...' or 'evaluate my summarize-changes skill with skill-creator'. Walks you through drafting SKILL.md, writing eval cases, running isolated subagent benchmarks, reviewing with eval-viewer/generate_review.py, grading, and tuning the description.
idioms: Frontmatter is just name (skill-creator) + description covering create/edit/optimize/eval/benchmark/description-tuning.
Evaluation-driven workflow: draft -> test prompts (with vs without skill) -> run + eval (qualitative + quantitative) -> rewrite -> repeat -> scale up; writes benchmark.json (pass rate, time, tokens).
Description-tuning loop generates should-trigger and should-not-trigger prompts to fix over/under-triggering.

Bundles scripts/ (packaging, benchmark aggregation, optimization loop), references/schemas.md, eval-viewer/generate_review.py, and agents/ subagent prompts; packages a finalized skill into a .skill file.
Docs note Claude understands SKILL.md natively, so this meta-skill is optional for authoring.
when_to_use: When creating a new skill from scratch, editing/optimizing an existing one, running evals, benchmarking skill performance with variance analysis, or tuning a description for triggering accuracy.

install: Open-source skill in anthropics/skills; copy skills/algorithmic-art/ into your skills dir.
quickstart: Ask: 'create generative art / a flow field / particle system'. Skill first writes an algorithmic-philosophy .md, then expresses it as p5.js .html + .js with seeded randomness and interactive parameters.
idioms: Two-step: (1) algorithmic philosophy .md, (2) p5.js generative art (.html viewer + .js algorithm).
Uses seeded randomness, noise fields, particles/flows/forces for emergent behavior.
Creates original art (does not copy existing artists) to avoid copyright issues.
when_to_use: When users request creating art with code: generative/algorithmic art, flow fields, or particle systems.

install: Open-source skill in anthropics/skills; copy skills/brand-guidelines/ into your skills dir.
quickstart: Ask to style an artifact 'with Anthropic brand colors/typography'. Skill applies Anthropic's official brand colors and font pairings to the artifact.
idioms: Applies Anthropic's official brand color palette and typography.
Triggered by keywords: branding, corporate/visual identity, brand colors, typography, visual formatting/post-processing.
when_to_use: When Anthropic brand colors, style guidelines, visual formatting, or company design standards should be applied to an artifact.

install: Open-source skill in anthropics/skills; copy skills/canvas-design/ into your skills dir.
quickstart: Ask: 'design a poster / piece of art'. Skill writes a visual design-philosophy .md, then produces static .png and/or .pdf output.
idioms: Two-step: design philosophy .md then express visually as .pdf/.png.
Focus on form, space, color, composition; minimal text as accent.
Creates original designs (no copying existing artists).
when_to_use: When the user asks to create a poster, art, design, or other static visual piece (output as .png/.pdf).

install: Bundled with Claude Code; also installable from anthropics/skills (skills/claude-api/).
quickstart: When building anything that calls Claude, the skill detects project language and points you at language-specific SDK docs for model ids, pricing, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, migration.
idioms: Reference for Claude API / Anthropic SDK across ~8 languages.
Strong TRIGGER block: fires on any Claude/Anthropic marker (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku, @anthropic-ai, claude-*) or LLM-shaped tasks with unstated provider.
SKIP override: if OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama is named or grep-detected, do not produce Anthropic SDK code.
Never answer LLM pricing/model/limits/caching questions from memory; read this skill.
when_to_use: Building or debugging LLM-powered apps with Claude; any question about Claude model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, caching, or migration.

install: Open-source skill in anthropics/skills; copy skills/doc-coauthoring/ into your skills dir.
quickstart: Ask to 'write a doc / draft a proposal / write a spec'. Skill runs a 3-stage workflow: Context Gathering, Refinement & Structure, Reader Testing.
idioms: Acts as an active guide through three stages, not a one-shot writer.
Triggers on doc types: PRD, design doc, decision doc, RFC, proposal, spec.
when_to_use: When the user wants to co-author documentation, proposals, technical specs, decision docs, or similar structured content.

install: Pre-built Agent Skill (skill_id `docx`, type anthropic) on API/claude.ai; also open-source in anthropics/skills.
quickstart: Read/analyze via pandoc; create new docs with docx-js; edit existing by unpack -> edit XML -> repack. Handles TOC, headings, page numbers, letterheads, images, find/replace, tracked changes, comments.
idioms: A .docx is a ZIP of XML files.
Create new = docx-js; edit existing = unpack/edit-XML/repack; read = pandoc or raw XML.
Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding.
when_to_use: Any task creating, reading, editing, or manipulating Word (.docx) documents, or producing a report/memo/letter/template as a Word file.

install: Open-source skill in anthropics/skills; copy skills/frontend-design/ into your skills dir.
quickstart: When building/redesigning UI, the skill pushes distinctive, non-templated aesthetic direction: deliberate palette, typography pairing, type scale, and one justified aesthetic risk grounded in the subject.
idioms: Avoid templated defaults (centered layouts, purple gradients, uniform rounded corners, Inter).
Ground design in a concrete subject/audience/page-job before designing.
Hero = a thesis; typography carries the page's personality.
when_to_use: When building new UI or reshaping existing UI and you want distinctive, intentional visual design (aesthetic direction, typography, layout).

install: Open-source skill in anthropics/skills; copy skills/internal-comms/ into your skills dir.
quickstart: Ask for a status report / leadership update / newsletter. Skill writes internal communications in the company's preferred formats.
idioms: Covers 3P updates (Progress/Plans/Problems), newsletters, FAQs, status/leadership/project updates, incident reports.
Uses company-preferred comms formats.
when_to_use: Whenever asked to write internal communications (status reports, leadership/3P/project updates, newsletters, FAQs, incident reports).

install: Open-source skill in anthropics/skills; copy skills/mcp-builder/ into your skills dir.
quickstart: Guides a 4-phase workflow to build high-quality MCP servers in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK), measuring quality by how well the tools let an LLM accomplish real tasks.
idioms: Supports Python FastMCP and Node/TypeScript MCP SDK.
Server quality = how well it enables LLMs to complete real-world tasks.
Structured four-phase development process.
when_to_use: When building an MCP server to integrate an external API or service (Python or Node/TS).

install: Pre-built Agent Skill (skill_id `pdf`, type anthropic) on API/claude.ai; also open-source in anthropics/skills.
quickstart: Python (pypdf/pdfplumber) + CLI tools for extract text/tables, merge/split, rotate, watermark, create, fill forms, encrypt/decrypt, extract images, OCR. See REFERENCE.md for advanced/JS; FORMS.md for form filling.
idioms: Quick start uses pypdf PdfReader/PdfWriter.
Advanced features + JS libraries live in REFERENCE.md; form filling in FORMS.md.
Covers OCR of scanned PDFs to make them searchable.
when_to_use: Any task involving PDF files: read/extract, merge/split, rotate, watermark, create, fill forms, encrypt/decrypt, extract images, or OCR.

install: Pre-built Agent Skill (skill_id `pptx`, type anthropic) on API/claude.ai; also open-source in anthropics/skills.
quickstart: Read via markitdown (`python -m markitdown presentation.pptx`); create from scratch with pptxgenjs (pptxgenjs.md); edit/create-from-template via editing.md. Handles decks, layouts, speaker notes, comments.
idioms: Read content with markitdown; create from scratch with pptxgenjs; edit/template via editing.md.
Trigger on 'deck', 'slides', 'presentation', or any .pptx filename regardless of downstream use.
when_to_use: Any time a .pptx is involved as input or output: create/edit decks, extract slide text, work with templates/layouts/notes/comments.

install: Open-source skill in anthropics/skills; copy skills/slack-gif-creator/ into your skills dir.
quickstart: Ask 'make me a Slack GIF of X doing Y'. Skill provides Slack size/FPS/color/duration constraints, animation concepts, and validation utilities.
idioms: Slack constraints: emoji 128x128, message 480x480, FPS 10-30, 48-128 colors, emoji GIFs < 3s.
Provides validation tools and animation concepts.
when_to_use: When users request animated GIFs optimized for Slack (e.g. 'make me a GIF of X doing Y for Slack').

install: Open-source skill in anthropics/skills; copy skills/theme-factory/ into your skills dir.
quickstart: Pick one of 10 pre-set color/font themes (or generate one on the fly) and apply consistent styling to an artifact (slides, docs, reports, HTML landing pages).
idioms: 10 curated themes, each a color palette + header/body font pairing.
Can also generate a new theme on the fly.
Applies after an artifact is created to give it a cohesive visual identity.
when_to_use: When styling an existing artifact (slides, docs, reports, HTML pages) with a consistent professional theme.

install: Open-source skill in anthropics/skills; copy skills/web-artifacts-builder/ into your skills dir.
quickstart: scripts/init-artifact.sh to scaffold, edit React+TS code, scripts/bundle-artifact.sh to bundle into a single HTML file for claude.ai. Stack: React 18 + TS + Vite + Parcel + Tailwind + shadcn/ui.
idioms: For complex multi-component artifacts needing state/routing/shadcn-ui, NOT simple single-file HTML/JSX.
Init -> develop -> bundle to single HTML -> display -> optionally test.
Avoid 'AI slop': no excessive centered layouts, purple gradients, uniform rounded corners, Inter font.
when_to_use: Creating elaborate, multi-component claude.ai HTML artifacts requiring state management, routing, or shadcn/ui components.

install: Open-source skill in anthropics/skills; copy skills/webapp-testing/ into your skills dir.
quickstart: Write native Python Playwright scripts; use scripts/with_server.py to manage server lifecycle. Always run helper scripts with --help first; treat them as black boxes (don't read source unless necessary).
idioms: Native Python Playwright; with_server.py manages one or more servers.
Decision tree starts with: is it static HTML? (read file for selectors) before driving a browser.
Run scripts with --help; avoid reading large helper-script source to save context.
when_to_use: Testing/interacting with local web apps: verify frontend behavior, debug UI, capture browser screenshots, view browser logs.

install: Pre-built Agent Skill (skill_id `xlsx`, type anthropic) on API/claude.ai; also open-source in anthropics/skills.
quickstart: Open/read/edit/fix or create .xlsx/.xlsm/.csv/.tsv: add columns, compute formulas, format, chart, clean messy data, convert tabular formats. Deliverable must be a spreadsheet file.
idioms: Use a consistent professional font; deliver with ZERO formula errors (#REF!,#DIV/0!,#VALUE!,#N/A,#NAME?).
When updating templates, EXACTLY match existing format/conventions (template conventions override guidelines).
Do NOT trigger when the real deliverable is a Word doc, HTML report, standalone script, DB pipeline, or Google Sheets integration.
when_to_use: Any task where a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output, including cleaning/restructuring messy tabular data into a proper spreadsheet.

tech: Claude API: Agent Skills (Skills API + container)
install: Send beta headers anthropic-beta: code-execution-2025-08-25,skills-2025-10-02,files-api-2025-04-14. Upload custom skills with curl -X POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/skills -F files[]=@SKILL.md ... (must include top-level SKILL.md, <30 MB).
quickstart: client.beta.messages.create(model=..., betas=["code-execution-2025-08-25","skills-2025-10-02"], container={"skills":[{"type":"anthropic","skill_id":"pptx","version":"latest"}]}, tools=[{"type":"code_execution_20250825","name":"code_execution"}], messages=[...])
idioms: Skills run inside the code-execution container; the code_execution tool MUST be included.
container.skills[] entry: type ('anthropic'|'custom'), skill_id, version ('latest' or pinned); up to 8 skills per request.
Pre-built skill_ids: pptx, xlsx, docx, pdf. Custom skills are workspace-wide; manage via GET/POST/DELETE /v1/skills and /v1/skills/{id}/versions.
API container has NO network access and NO runtime package installs (pre-installed packages only); API versions are epoch timestamps (e.g. 1759178010641129) or 'latest'.
allowed-tools frontmatter is IGNORED on the API/SDK; use the request tool config and permissions. Skills are not ZDR-eligible.
when_to_use: When integrating Skills into your own application/agent through the Claude Messages API on the developer platform (or AWS/Microsoft Foundry).

install: npm i @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk (TS) or pip install claude-agent-sdk (Python). Skills are filesystem artifacts under .claude/skills/ or ~/.claude/skills/.
quickstart: options = ClaudeAgentOptions(cwd="/path/to/project", setting_sources=["user","project"], skills="all", allowed_tools=["Read","Write","Bash","Skill"])  # TS: { settingSources:["user","project"], skills:"all", allowedTools:[...] }
idioms: Skills are discovered via setting sources: include 'user' and/or 'project' in setting_sources/settingSources or skills will not load.
skills option filters: 'all', a list of names, or [] to disable; it is a context filter, NOT a sandbox (files still reachable via Read/Bash).
Setting skills auto-adds the 'Skill' tool to allowedTools; if you pass explicit tools, include "Skill".
No programmatic skill-registration API — skills must be filesystem artifacts (unlike subagents); load from a path via the plugins option.
allowed-tools frontmatter does NOT apply in the SDK; control tools via allowedTools + canUseTool + permissionMode.
when_to_use: When a custom agent should discover and auto-invoke filesystem Skills programmatically; the supported way to build a Claude-based agent with a Skills mechanism.

install: mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/<name> (personal) or .claude/skills/<name> (project); add SKILL.md. Plugins: /plugin marketplace add anthropics/skills then /plugin install ...
quickstart: ---; description: Summarizes uncommitted changes and flags anything risky. Use when the user asks what changed or wants a commit message.; ---; ## Current changes; !`git diff HEAD`; ## Instructions; Summarize the changes above — invoke with /summarize-changes or let Claude auto-trigger.
idioms: Custom commands merged into skills: .claude/commands/x.md and .claude/skills/x/SKILL.md both create /x; skill wins on name clash.
Override order: enterprise > personal > project; plugin skills are namespaced (plugin:skill) and never conflict; nested monorepo skills get dir-qualified names (apps/web:deploy).
Invocation control: disable-model-invocation: true (manual only), user-invocable: false (Claude only); restrict via Skill(name)/Skill(name *) permission rules or skillOverrides setting.
allowed-tools pre-approves tools without restricting availability (gated by workspace trust dialog for project skills); disallowed-tools removes tools for the turn.

Dynamic context injection with !`cmd` runs before Claude sees the skill; context: fork runs the skill in a subagent; extra frontmatter: when_to_use, argument-hint, arguments, model, effort, paths, shell, hooks; $ARGUMENTS/$N and ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} substitutions.

Skill names always listed but descriptions truncated to a budget (default 1% of context, <=1536 chars/entry); tune via skillListingBudgetFraction / SLASH_COMMAND_TOOL_CHAR_BUDGET / maxSkillDescriptionChars; /doctor reports dropped descriptions.
when_to_use: When creating, scoping, or governing Skills/slash-commands in the Claude Code CLI, including team-shared project skills and plugin distribution.
